deep_dive.title: x-Prediction Is All You Need:Training-Free Accelerated Generation via Endpoint Decodability
deep_dive.abstract: Diffusion and flow matching models generate high-quality samples, but their ODE samplers often need tens to hundreds of neural function evaluations (NFEs). This remains a practical challenge for released checkpoints, since many accelerators require additional design choices and training cost through retraining, distillation, or trajectory redesign. We investigate a different route based on $x$-prediction. During sampling, standard affine probability paths already expose $x_0$ information: an intermediate state and its path velocity determine a principled estimate of the clean sample. We formalize this property as \textbf{endpoint decodability} and show that the decoder is the minimum-MSE estimator $\mathbb{E}[x_0\mid x_t]$ under the usual $\ell_2$ objective. This yields \textbf{Truncated Jump Sampling} (TJS): stop the ODE at an early-exit time $t^*$ and return the decoded $x_0$. TJS requires no retraining, distillation, or architecture change. Across SDXL, SD3.5M, Z-Image-Turbo, and three class-conditional benchmarks, it reduces NFEs by 20--70\% with near-matched quality. The analysis also shows why endpoint prediction can work without straightening the trajectory, providing inference acceleration without trajectory redesign.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: proposes training-free accelerated generation via endpoint decodability for diffusion and flow models
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 提出通过端点可解码性实现扩散和流模型的免训练加速生成
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 提出通过端点可解码性实现扩散和流模型的免训练加速生成
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 提出通过端点可解码性实现扩散和流模型的免训练加速生成
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: x-Prediction introduces endpoint decodability for training-free accelerated diffusion sampling, directly optimizing inference without retraining.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对扩散模型需要大量函数评估的问题，提出基于x预测的端点可解码性，实现免训练加速生成。该方法利用中间状态和速度直接估计干净样本（x0），无需重训练或蒸馏，显著减少采样步数。它直接提供了免训练优化技术，完美匹配用户需求。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 本文针对扩散模型需要大量函数评估的问题，提出基于x预测的端点可解码性，实现免训练加速生成。该方法利用中间状态和速度直接估计干净样本（x0），无需重训练或蒸馏，显著减少采样步数。它直接提供了免训练优化技术，完美匹配用户需求。

quick_skim.title: Wan-Dancer: A Hierarchical Framework for Minute-scale Coherent Music-to-Dance Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Generating long-duration, high-definition, and rhythmically synchronized dance videos directly from music remains a significant challenge, primarily due to the temporal constraints of current diffusion models, which typically fail beyond 20 seconds. Existing approaches, whether they rely on intermediate 3D skeletons or on end-to-end video synthesis, suffer from temporal drift, identity inconsistency, and repetitive motion patterns when extended to longer horizons. To address these limitations, we propose a novel hierarchical framework for minute-scale coherent music-to-dance generation. Our method decouples the process into global keyframe planning and local temporal refinement, leveraging full-track musical context to ensure long-range coherence. Key innovations include dynamic frame rate adaptation via time-mapped RoPE embeddings for precise alignment, an optical-flow-based loss function to enhance motion continuity, and motion-speed control to preserve high-fidelity details during rapid movements. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our framework surpasses the conventional duration barrier, generating stable, 720p/30fps videos exceeding one minute with superior temporal stability. Furthermore, the model exhibits robust versatility across five distinct dance genres, conditioned on both audio and textual prompts, establishing a new state-of-the-art in coherent, long-form dance video synthesis.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes hierarchical framework for minute-scale coherent music-to-dance video generation using diffusion models
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出分层框架用于分钟级连贯音乐到舞蹈视频生成，使用扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出分层框架用于分钟级连贯音乐到舞蹈视频生成，使用扩散模型
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出分层框架用于分钟级连贯音乐到舞蹈视频生成，使用扩散模型
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Wan-Dancer generates long-duration dance videos with diffusion models via global keyframe planning and local temporal refinement, addressing temporal drift.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文针对扩散模型难以生成超过20秒长视频的问题，提出Wan-Dancer分层框架。它通过全局关键帧规划和局部时间细化，结合完整音乐上下文，生成长达分钟级的高清、节奏同步舞蹈视频。方法有效抑制了时间漂移和身份不一致，但未强调免训练优化，属于视频生成方法改进。

quick_skim.title: Asymptotic-Preserving A Posteriori Analysis of Diffusion and Flow-Matching Samplers
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion and flow-matching samplers integrate a learned probability-flow ODE from a large noise scale down to a small terminal floor $σ_{\min}$, at which the score is stiff and the flow develops a boundary layer. We treat $σ_{\min}$ as a singular-perturbation parameter and determine which fixed-step samplers are asymptotic-preserving (AP), that is, stable and uniformly accurate as $σ_{\min}\to0$, casting the criteria as an a posteriori audit: residual functionals with $σ_{\min}$-uniform coefficients, computable on a pretrained checkpoint without ground-truth scores or exact trajectories. On the terminal layer, Euler in the $σ$-clock, the deterministic DDIM update, is the unique layer-exact discretization up to affine reparameterization, with rectified flow its flow-matching counterpart; the $λ$-clock is stable only for steps $h\le h_\star=1+W(1/e)$, and the uniform-$σ^2$ heat clock stalls a $σ_{\min}$-independent distance from the data. On two solvable models (rank-deficient Gaussian, symmetric two-point mixture), deterministic samplers remain first-order uniformly accurate with no $\log(1/σ_{\min})$ factor, even across a symmetric posterior-switching interface whose distributional budget is a universal constant; the logarithm is charged entirely to the Itô term of stochastic samplers, whose path-KL scales as $Λ^2/N$ against the ODE's $O(Λ^2/N^2)$ budget, with $Λ=\log(σ_{\max}/σ_{\min})$. On the EDM CIFAR-10 checkpoint, spectra measured once predict held-out residual budgets across step count, schedule, and noise level against pre-specified gates with no per-configuration refitting, and calibrate the Itô coefficient at $M_1=1.00\pm0.01$. The clock decides stability; the noise, not the geometry, charges the logarithm.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: analyzes diffusion samplers without requiring ground-truth scores, enabling a posteriori audit
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 分析扩散采样器，无需真实分数，支持事后审计
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 分析扩散采样器，无需真实分数，支持事后审计
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 分析扩散采样器，无需真实分数，支持事后审计
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Provides asymptotic-preserving analysis and a posteriori audit for diffusion and flow-matching samplers, computable without ground-truth scores or exact trajectories.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散和流匹配采样器在终端层面临数值困难，本文将其作为奇异摄动问题处理，确定了哪些固定步长采样器具有渐近保持性质（稳定且均匀精确）。通过提出一种基于残余泛函的后验审计方法，可以在预训练检查点上直接计算，无需真实分数或精确轨迹。该方法为评估和选择采样器提供了免训练的分析工具，有助于优化扩散模型采样。

quick_skim.title: MobileWan: Closing the Quality Gap for Mobile Video Diffusion
quick_skim.abstract: Recent advances in video diffusion have been driven by scaling transformer-based architectures to billions of parameters, substantially improving visual fidelity and motion coherence. In contrast, existing mobile video diffusion models remain limited to relatively small parameter budgets, typically 0.4-1.8B, restricting generation quality. In this work, we show that high-quality mobile video generation does not require small models. Instead, we demonstrate that a server-scale 5B-parameter video diffusion transformer can be deployed efficiently on memory-constrained mobile hardware through recurrent reformulation and structured compression. Starting from Wan2.2-5B, we rely on a recurrence distillation framework that converts video generation into a chunk-wise autoregressive process with constant-memory attention computation. Combined with causal linear attention, the model operates as an RNN at inference time while preserving temporal coherence across chunks. We further propose a learnable attention head pruning method based on binary per-head gates optimized end-to-end using a noise-biased sparsity objective and distillation-based finetuning. Together with sampling-step distillation and memory-optimized VAE decoding, MobileWan becomes the first 5B-scale video diffusion model deployable on a commercial mobile device. Our system generates 5-second 480x832 videos at 16 FPS in 20 seconds end-to-end latency, achieving a VBench score of 83.79 and establishing a new state of the art in mobile video generation. Project page: https://qualcomm-ai-research.github.io/mobilewan
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: video diffusion model deployment on mobile through compression
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过压缩在移动端部署视频扩散模型

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: MobileWan deploys a 5B-parameter video diffusion transformer on mobile via recurrence distillation and structured compression.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文旨在解决移动设备上视频扩散模型质量差的问题，提出通过递归蒸馏和结构化压缩，将服务器级5B参数视频扩散变压器高效部署到移动端。该方法虽需蒸馏训练，但显著提升了移动端视频生成质量，缩小了与服务器的差距。

quick_skim.title: D2PO: Optimizing Diffusion Samplers via Dynamic Preference
quick_skim.abstract: We propose D2PO (Dynamic Direct Preference Optimization), a principled framework for optimizing diffusion sampling policies with respect to timestep schedules and classifier-free guidance (CFG) weights. Our work is motivated by a fundamental limitation of existing student-teacher regression frameworks; low-NFE student samplers are trained to mimic high-NFEteachers, often sacrificing high-frequency texture fidelity while preserving coarse global structures, thereby misaligning the sampler with perceptual quality. D2PO addresses this challenge by reformulating sampler optimization as a preference-based alignment problem, leveraging the Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) framework. To make DPO applicable to diffusion samplers, we model the sampling policy as an energy-based model (EBM), transforming preference comparisons into tractable energy differences. We further introduce a novel energy formulation derived directly from the pretrained score network, enabling preference evaluation in perturbed spaces that jointly capture structural consistency and fine-grained details. Moreover, we introduce dynamic preferences, where the preferred samples used for alignment progressively improve as the sampling policies are learned. This self-improving mechanism replaces rigid static teacher supervision with an iterative, preference-guided refinement process, providing progressively stronger alignment signals. Extensive experiments demonstrate that D2PO aligns diffusion samplers with perceptual quality more faithfully, unlocking the full potential of high-quality teachers and consistently outperforming conventional regression-based schedulers under low-NFE constraints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimizing diffusion sampler policies via dynamic preference optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过动态偏好优化扩散采样器策略
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过动态偏好优化扩散采样器策略
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过动态偏好优化扩散采样器策略
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: D2PO optimizes timestep schedules and CFG weights for diffusion samplers using dynamic direct preference optimization, improving perceptual quality.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对低NFE学生采样器牺牲高频纹理的问题，提出D2PO框架，将采样器优化建模为偏好对齐任务，利用直接偏好优化调整时间步调度和分类器自由引导权重。该方法无需重新训练生成模型，可视为训练后优化策略。

quick_skim.title: Tensor Train Diffusion: Leveraging Low-Rank Structures for High-Dimensional Score-Based Sampling
quick_skim.abstract: Diffusion models offer a powerful framework for sampling from complex probability densities by learning to reverse a noising process. A common approach involves solving for the time-reversed stochastic differential equation (SDE), which requires the score function of the evolving sample distribution. The logarithm of this distribution's density is governed by a Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman (HJB) type partial differential equation (PDE). However, current methods for solving this PDE, such as PINNs or trajectory-based techniques, often suffer from long training times and significant sensitivity to hyperparameter tuning. In this work, we introduce a novel and efficient solver for the underlying HJB equation based on the functional tensor train (FTT) format. The FTT representation leverages latent low-rank structures to efficiently approximate high-dimensional functions, enabling both model compression and rapid computation. By integrating this efficient representation with a backward-in-time iterative scheme derived from backward stochastic differential equations (BSDEs), we develop a fast, robust and accurate sampling method. Our approach overcomes primary bottlenecks of existing techniques, enabling high-fidelity sampling from challenging target distributions with improved efficiency.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: efficient solver for score function PDE without long training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 高效的分数函数PDE求解器，无需长时间训练
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 高效的分数函数PDE求解器，无需长时间训练
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 高效的分数函数PDE求解器，无需长时间训练
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Tensor Train Diffusion solves the HJB equation for score-based sampling using functional tensor train, avoiding lengthy training of PINNs.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 扩散模型的分数函数求解通常需训练PINNs或轨迹方法，时间长且超参数敏感。本文提出基于函数张量训练的HJB方程求解器，利用低秩结构高效计算分数，无需长时间训练。在多种高维分布采样任务上，该方法在采样质量和效率上均优于现有方法。
